programmes.show_description: Rumpole and the Case of Identity: Can Rumpole successfully prove that his client is the victim of mistaken identity?
Rumpole and the Show Folk: Rumpole travels to the north of England to defend an actress in a murder trial.
Open House: Henry returns home early from a business trip to discover that Matthew has thrown a house party without his permission.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
One Winged Angels: Jonathan has a tough time on an assignment when he falls in love with the woman he's supposed to be pairing up with another man.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Going Home, Going Home: God grants Mark the chance to go back in time and tell his grandfather that he loved him.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.

Teleshopping.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Hero: John-Boy decides to create a memorial in which Grandma Walton will be honoured.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Inferno: John-Boy is deeply affected when he witnesses the crash of the Hindenburg. Meanwhile, Curt tries to find a way to be alone with Mary Ellen.
The Heartbreaker: Jason Walton learns that love can be one of life's most painful experiences.
The Long Night: With Grandma in the hospital, Grandpa feels alone and unneeded. But will an unlikely, blossoming friendship be enough to lift his spirits?
The Hiding Place: The Baldwins' niece Hilary arrives on Walton's Mountain, seeking a hiding place from the horrors of war breaking out in her native Austria.
The Go-Getter: Ben's tactics for selling used cars alienates both family and friends.
The Achievement: John-Boy travels to New York in pursuit of his dreams.
The Hawk: A fierce hawk threatens the community, and a handsome new minister comes to Walton's Mountain.
The Stray: Mysterious events leads to the discovery of a 12-year-old orphan boy, who attaches himself to John Walton.
Fantasy with Brad Pitt and Anthony Hopkins. In exchange for prolonging his life, an ailing tycoon agrees to be Death's guide to the mortal world, where romance and other discoveries await. (1998)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Fantasy with Brad Pitt and Anthony Hopkins. In exchange for prolonging his life, an ailing tycoon agrees to be Death's guide to the mortal world, where romance and other discoveries await. (1998)
Revolutionary epic with Mel Gibson, Heath Ledger and Joely Richardson. It's muskets out and tricorns on as war hero Benjamin Martin defends his home, family and nation from the Brits... (2000)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Revolutionary epic with Mel Gibson, Heath Ledger and Joely Richardson. It's muskets out and tricorns on as war hero Benjamin Martin defends his home, family and nation from the Brits... (2000)
Der Tag: Hawkeye and BJ place a toe tag on a passed-out Frank as part of a drunken practical joke, but the joke backfires on them when he is accidentally shipped out to the front lines.
Hawkeye: Hawkeye is rescued by a Korean family when he is injured in a jeep accident. He knows how dangerous it can be to fall asleep with a concussion, so he talks to the family non-stop.
Some 38th Parallels: Frank becomes increasingly paranoid that the locals are stealing the camp's garbage, so he decides to auction off 150 pounds of it.
The Novocaine Mutiny: Frank charges Hawkeye with mutiny, but they each have different views of the circumstances leading to the court-martial.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Acting out: Matthew decides he wants to become an actor, but Henry is concerned his son will make a fool of himself during a college production.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
As Difficult As ABC: Brian Baldwin, a college basketball star, is diagnosed with cardiac insufficiency. After losing his scholarship he's forced to deal with his illiteracy.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
A Child Of God: Jonathan and Mark try to reunite Marsha with her father but he refuses to see her for having a child out of wedlock. But Marsha doesn't have much time left on Earth.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
The Return Of The Masked Rider: Jonathan and Mark help ageing movie heroes confront the gang, Satan's Helpers. They must persuade a boxer not to throw a big fight when the gang bullies him.
Song Of The Wild West: Jonathan and Mark visit a country-western bar where they meet a singer hoping to follow in her mother's footsteps. A broken family also needs Jonathan's assistance.
The Pride of Walnut Grove: Mary worries that she will disappoint Walnut Grove if she doesn't place first in the state maths competition after the community pays her way to represent them.
A Matter of Faith: Caroline sends her family on ahead to the picnic grounds for the weekend while she nurses a nasty cut to her leg that becomes infected.
The Runaway Caboose: At a train station, Mary, Laura and Carl wander into a caboose. Without warning, the caboose begins to move, and it is soon on a crash course with an oncoming train.
The Recluse: Jason befriends a mysterious recluse who loves music, and Ben leaves home to look for a new job.
The Warrior: The startling history of Walton's Mountain leads to the discovery of an ancient burial ground.
Catch of the Day: While sand and mud create earthy problems around the village, Niamh takes a radical decision to solve a problem of her own.
Something Old: Jean and Eddie's big day will certainly be one they will never forget! Meanwhile, Tom considers visiting his father in New Zealand, and Donald tries to help a young orphan.
Eleanor is furious when Lady Cora arranges a meeting between the Thrift and Sir Herbert Hamilton and the governors of the East London Hospital.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Genuine Article: Rumpole defends an unknown artist who is accused of forging a famous painting.
Mulcahy's War: Frank claims a patient shot himself to avoid combat and calls Father Mulcahy. Troubled by his lack of experience at the front, he accompanies Radar to an aid station there.
The Korean Surgeon: Instead of turning him in as a prisoner of war, Hawkeye and B.J. pull some strings to get a wounded North Korean surgeon a spot on the staff.
Hawkeye Get Your Gun: Hawkeye and Potter provide assistance at a Korean clinic, and run into an enemy attack on the way home. Klinger poses as a Gypsy to help get his discharge papers.
The Colonel's Horse: Col. Potter leaves for some R&R and asks Radar to look after his mare. Margaret asks Hawkeye to operate on her appendix.
Hawk's Nightmare: Havoc ensues when Radar removes a spirit post from the road. When a man is brought in for medical attention, he refuses surgery unless the camp spirits are exorcised.
Exorcism: Hawkeye is surprised to learn that he has been sleepwalking. All he knows is that he's been particularly depressed about the young age of the soldiers he is trying to keep alive.
Unforgettable Characters: Radar becomes an aspiring writer and begins writing his impressions of the camp. Hawkeye and BJ give Frank a surprise birthday present that only he will appreciate.
